violations.description: Person in charge. — --Certified food service manager had an expired certificate or is no longer associated with this establishment. Could not verify if any of the employees have a current ServeSafe certificate.

Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. Several open food boxes being stored on the floor of the walk-in freezer.

General equipment and utensils - Maintenance and operation. — --Nonfood contact equipment/utensil was not maintained in a good repair or sound condition. Shelving in the kitchen is lined with cardboard and has become saturated with grease, cardboard is not a cleanable surface. Shelving in the dry storage has water damaged and heavy pocking on the particle board shelves.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Inside of the microwave in the kitchen needs to be cleaned.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Side of cooking equipment, racking, and shelving are all in need a more thorough cleaning.

Mechanical cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --An approved sanitizer was not used during the mechanical warewashing operation. Chemical dish machine that uses bleach as a sanitizer was connected to a quat based chemical bottle. Machine was reading at 0ppm for any sanitizer.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored, handled, dispensed in a manner to protect from contamination. Single service items being stored under cleaning chemicals in the dry storage.

Handwashing lavatory supplies. — --Handwash lavatory was not supplied with supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder or bar soap. Soap dispenser on the prep line was not working at the time of inspection.--Handwash lavatory was not supplied with supply of individual disposable towels, continuous towel supply system, or heated air-drying device. Hand towel dispenser was empty at the time of inspection, waiting for delivery to refill the dispenser.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Floors, walls, and/or ceiling were not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent, and easily cleanable. Bare wood OSB wall separating the kitchen and the lottery room, need to be sealed to become a cleanable surface.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Floors throughout the facility need a more thorough cleaning. Walls in the kitchen and food prep areas need cleaned.

Storage and handling of maintenance items. — --Wet-use maintenance items, such as mops, were not placed in a position that allows proper air-drying. Wet mops stored in the mop sink were not placed in a proper position to dry.

Handling and storage of poisonous and toxic materials. — --Poisonous or toxic material was not stored in a manner to protect against contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-service items. Chemical sprayer container "concrete-asphalt degreaser" was stored next to the metal pizza pans in the kitchen.
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Available, knowledgeable, certified manager (4 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pt deducted)
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Clean/dry location, protected, covered, original containers, labeled (2 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (2 pts deducted)
